creator: jrm@freebsd.org
text:
Sorry, that ldd output was not correct.  I had accidentally left in some code that I was testing.  This is what it should look like.

% ldd /usr/local/lib/R/library/stats/libs/stats.so
/usr/local/lib/R/library/stats/libs/stats.so:
        libopenblasp.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libopenblasp.so.0 (0x801400000)
        libgfortran.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/gcc5/libgfortran.so.3 (0x80322f000)
        libm.so.5 => /lib/libm.so.5 (0x803551000)
        libquadmath.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/gcc5/libquadmath.so.0 (0x80377c000)
        libR.so => not found (0)
        libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x800823000)
        libthr.so.3 => /lib/libthr.so.3 (0x8039bb000)
        libgcc_s.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/gcc5/libgcc_s.so.1 (0x803be2000)

The "not found" for libR.so is expected, because it's under ${PREFIX}/lib/R/lib and opened internally by R with dlopen(3).  That being said, I think we could make everyone happy if we get an SONAME with a version number on these libraries (see bug #219776).  After that we could create links under ${{PREFIX}/lib.
